mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeslice on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeSlice provides secure and highly available connectivity between multiple Kubernetes clusters by creating an overlay network. Installing KubeSlice allows applications to communicate seamlessly across cluster boundaries, making it ideal for multi-cloud and multi-cluster deployments.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the KubeSlice Helm repository to your local Helm setup using the following command:
```console
helm repo add kubeslice https://kubeslice.github.io/kubeslice/
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KubeSlice
steps.description:
Install KubeSlice with the following command, specifying the version and creating a namespace:
```console
helm install kubeslice kubeslice/kubeslice --version kubeslice-worker-1.5.0 --namespace kubeslice --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the KubeSlice pods are running:
```console
kubectl get pods -n kubeslice
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the KubeSlice values to set resource limits. Create a `values.yaml` file with the following content:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```
Then apply the configuration:
```console
helm upgrade kubeslice kubeslice/kubeslice --namespace kubeslice -f values.yaml
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC
steps.description:
Ensure that RBAC is enabled for KubeSlice by adding the following to your `values.yaml`:
```yaml
rbac:
  create: true
```
Then upgrade the installation to apply the RBAC settings:
```console
helm upgrade kubeslice kubeslice/kubeslice --namespace kubeslice -f values.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the KubeSlice release using the following command:
```console
helm uninstall kubeslice --namespace kubeslice
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) created by KubeSlice:
```console
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep kubeslice | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the KubeSlice namespace to clean up all resources:
```console
kubectl delete namespace kubeslice
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration:
```console
helm get values kubeslice --namespace kubeslice > backup-values.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date:
```console
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeSlice
upgrade.description:
Upgrade KubeSlice to the latest version:
```console
helm upgrade kubeslice kubeslice/kubeslice --namespace kubeslice --version kubeslice-worker-1.5.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the KubeSlice pods are running after the upgrade:
```console
kubectl get pods -n kubeslice
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources:
```console
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n kubeslice
```
Ensure your cluster has enough resources available or adjust resource requests/limits.

troubleshooting.title: KubeSlice pods crash looping
troubleshooting.description:
If KubeSlice pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs:
```console
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeslice
```
Investigate the logs for errors and adjust configurations as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect between clusters
troubleshooting.description:
If connectivity issues arise, ensure that the overlay network is correctly configured. Check the KubeSlice configuration:
```console
kubectl get kubeslice -n kubeslice
```
Review the settings and ensure all clusters are properly connected.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeSlice will have all pods running in the 'kubeslice' namespace, and you will be able to verify connectivity between clusters. The configuration for resource limits and RBAC should be applied correctly.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubeslice https://kubeslice.github.io/kubeslice/
helm repo update
helm install kubeslice kubeslice/kubeslice --version kubeslice-worker-1.5.0 --namespace kubeslice --create-namespace

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
helm upgrade kubeslice kubeslice/kubeslice --namespace kubeslice -f values.yaml

prerequisites.description: Ensure that Helm and kubectl are installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.
